behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at the boundary between range resolution and function evaluation (off-by-one ranges, type coercion across cells), which a mocked unit of SUM alone can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with values in A1:A10, set one cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run the real parser + reference resolver + evaluator path, and assert the computed value.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This behavior’s failure boundary is inter-cell dependency wiring (formula-to-formula chaining), not a single local function.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to a value, B1 to `=A1*2`, C1 to `=B1+1`; evaluate workbook using real dependency resolution and assert C1 matches cascaded math.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a graph-level interaction across multiple cells/nodes; unit-testing one evaluator function can hide recursion/graph traversal defects.
behaviors.test_description: Construct A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`, execute normal recalculation, and assert a circular-reference error object/code is returned quickly (no hang).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Minimum realistic failure boundary includes table range extraction plus VLOOKUP semantics; mocking either side can hide key-not-found and column-index issues.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table in cells, evaluate `VLOOKUP(..., FALSE)` through the real formula engine for both found and missing keys, and assert value vs error outcomes.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is expression evaluation/branching logic within one evaluator boundary; no external module boundary is required to expose failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the expression evaluator on nested IF AST/formula with representative A1 values (<=10, 11-20, >20) and assert returned branch strings.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: This is function-level string assembly behavior; failures are in local CONCATENATE implementation rules, not infrastructure wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate CONCATENATE inputs including empty/null cell values in a local evaluator test and assert separator placement and skipped-empty behavior.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet resolution depends on workbook/sheet namespace wiring; unit tests of arithmetic alone cannot catch sheet lookup failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1, evaluate Sheet1 formula `=Sheet2!A1+1` via real reference resolver, assert result.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears in dependency graph update + scheduler interaction; mocking one component can falsely pass while order is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond across cells, evaluate once, mutate an upstream cell, trigger incremental recalc, and assert all downstream cells match expected values after topological recomputation.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness spans history stack, formula storage, and recalculation engine; single-function tests miss cross-module state restoration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that affects dependents, capture pre-edit state, perform edit and recalc, execute undo through real command/history path, then assert restored formula text and dependent computed values.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Real serializer/deserializer plus filesystem interaction is required; mocks can hide binary format drift and float round-trip issues.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook containing formulas and high-precision floats to a real temp file in the custom binary format, reload it via normal load path, and assert exact formula text plus numeric equality within defined precision policy.
